answers.motivation: Dev shortcut — testing the facial analysis pipeline end to end.

metrics.curvatureDescription: Slightly flatter than the common curvature range
metrics.explanation: Your eyes show negative (downturned) canthal tilt with moderate eyelid exposure. Sclera reads as yellow-tinged with good under-eye appearance. Lower eyelid curvature (0.79) is slightly flatter than the common curvature range — typical bending range is 0.76–0.92.

faceShape.explanation: Based on the subject's facial landmark measurements, the subject's face shape is classified as round with a width-to-height ratio of 1.02. This shape provides a soft foundation for hairstyle and styling recommendations.

symmetry.explanation: The subject's facial symmetry score of 58 reflects noticeable left-right variation in several feature pairs. This is still within the range seen in everyday faces.

proportions.explanation: Vertical facial thirds — upper 0.15, middle 0.60, lower 0.26.

nasoAural.expectation: Generally, the ears are expected to be roughly the same height as the nose.
nasoAural.explanation: Your ears are shorter relative to nose height on profile, which can make the midface appear more prominent from the side. This is common and well within natural variation.

orbitoNasal.expectation: Generally, the outer edges of the nostrils are expected to align with the inner corners of the eyes.
orbitoNasal.explanation: The subject's broader nasal base relative to inner eye spacing gives the center of the subject's face a strong vertical column.

nasoOral.expectation: Generally, the width of the mouth is expected to be about 50–60% wider than the width of the nasal base.
nasoOral.explanation: The subject's comparatively wide nasal base with a more contained mouth width places structural emphasis on the nose.

orbital.expectation: Generally, the space between the eyes is expected to be equal to the width of one eye.
orbital.explanation: The subject's eye size is slightly wider than the gap between them so the subject's eyes read as somewhat wide-set, providing a regular reference for judging the size of the subject's nose and mouth.

nose.explanation: The subject's nose displays a wide alar base with a nose-to-face ratio of 0.20. The width-to-length ratio of 1.55 suggests a well-proportioned nasal structure relative to the subject's facial dimensions. Profile angles: nasofrontal 168.9°, nasolabial 97.4° (typical 90–110°), dorsal hump minimal.

eyebrows.explanation: The subject's brows sit in a mid set position with a soft arch form and upturned tilt. The thick brow structure with a short tail length contributes to a balanced periorbital frame. Inner brows are parallel with gentle taper taper.

eyelashes.explanation: Lash-line contrast index 229 suggests dense apparent density with dark lash pigmentation on the frontal photo.

ocular.curvatureDescription: Slightly flatter than the common curvature range
ocular.explanation: Your eyes show negative (downturned) canthal tilt with moderate eyelid exposure. Sclera reads as yellow-tinged with good under-eye appearance. Lower eyelid curvature (0.79) is slightly flatter than the common curvature range — typical bending range is 0.76–0.92.

underEye.explanation: Under-eye brightness 161 with noticeable pigmentation signal; hollowing reads as minimal hollow on the frontal photo.

metrics.explanation: The subject's brows sit in a mid set position with a soft arch form and upturned tilt. The thick brow structure with a short tail length contributes to a balanced periorbital frame. Inner brows are parallel with gentle taper taper.

lips.explanation: The subject's lips show full volume with a 2075.29 philtrum-to-lip ratio and flat cupid's bow definition. The upper-to-lower lip proportion is longer philtrum, contributing to a harmonious perioral appearance.

jawChin.explanation: The subject's jaw displays a round contour with a face width-to-height ratio of 1.02. The chin appears prominent with a jaw angle of 87.4° — creating a softer lower facial contour.

cheeks.explanation: The subject's cheeks display wide width with high cheekbone placement. The cheekbones appear prominent with a angular jaw-to-cheek transition. Midface is balanced (19.6% of face height). Cheek symmetry is noticeably asymmetric (asymmetry: 2.58%).

jaw.explanation: The subject's jaw spans 99.9% of facial width (Wide) with a angular mandibular angle of 87.4°. The jawline shows smooth contour smoothness and soft definition. Jaw length is long at 74.2% of face height.

chin.explanation: The subject's chin is long (27.0% of face height) with prominent projection. The chin is narrow (20.5% of face width) with a soft square shape. The labiomental fold shows a open angle of 172.9°, contributing to lower facial balance.

smile.explanation: Smile analysis: minimal teeth visibility with consonant (ideal u-shape). Gum exposure is gummy smile. Teeth appear natural.

neck.explanation: The subject's neck proportions show wide width (143.4% of IPD) and long length (44.5% of face height from jaw to shoulder line). The jaw-to-neck transition is defined at 137.0°. Head posture appears neutral (-1.0° from vertical).

ears.explanation: The subject's ears are small (0.31× IPD) and asymmetric (diff: 21.0%). Protrusion is protruding with mid-set positioning. Ear prominence affects the overall silhouette.

hair.explanation: Hair analysis from top-of-head photo: thick hair with full coverage. Estimated Norwood stage 2 (not a clinical diagnosis). Hairline is slight recession with moderate forehead exposure.

skin.explanation: Forehead brightness 126 · cheek redness index 33.1 · tone uniformity varied. Under-eye region shows shadowed appearance. Tone appears noticeably uneven. Mild redness detected — consider anti-inflammatory skincare.

dimorphism.explanation: The subject's face reads as moderate with an round overall shape, a relatively balanced upper third, structured midface, and a strong jaw and chin. Strong brows, nasal structure, and lateral framing from ears and neck reinforce a clear moderate facial configuration.

subsections.title: Hair Style
subsections.body: The subject's hair and scalp analysis shows score 83/100 (Natural), hairline: Slight recession, densityEstimate: Thick. Styles that balance facial thirds and keep the forehead and temples tidy without aggressive chemical processing are appropriate. Respect contraindication flags; avoid actives that conflict with reported allergies, infections, or recent retinoids. Reassess under consistent lighting after 30 days.

subsections.title: Hair Loss
subsections.body: Based on the subject's scalp and density findings (score 83/100 (Natural), hairline: Slight recession, densityEstimate: Thick), focus on conservative maintenance rather than aggressive claims. Respect contraindication flags; avoid actives that conflict with reported allergies, infections, or recent retinoids. Gentle cleansing, avoiding excessive heat, and discussing persistent thinning with a dermatologist are appropriate next steps. This estimate is educational, not a clinical diagnosis.

protocolData.summary: A personalized non-surgical protocol based on the subject's stored facial analysis scores.

recommendations.title: Facial muscle exercises
recommendations.description: Jawline and neck stretches for 10 minutes daily can support lower-face definition alongside posture work.

recommendations.title: Sleep position awareness
recommendations.description: Back sleeping reduces overnight facial pressure that can accentuate asymmetry over time.

recommendations.title: Skincare routine optimization
recommendations.description: Daily SPF 30+, gentle cleanser, and a consistent evening moisturizer support measurable skin quality gains.

recommendations.title: Daily sunscreen
recommendations.description: Broad-spectrum SPF 30+ every morning is the highest-impact non-surgical anti-aging habit.

recommendations.title: Hydration and nutrition
recommendations.description: Adequate water, omega-3s, and reduced processed sugar support skin clarity from within.

recommendations.title: Quality sleep
recommendations.description: Seven to nine hours of consistent sleep supports recovery, under-eye appearance, and skin repair.

protocolNarrative.summary: This evidence-based non-surgical protocol is grounded in the subject's measured facial analysis (overall harmony 77/100), organised around key aesthetic features.
closing: The subject's assessment shows an overall harmony score of 77/100 from facial measurements. Treat the score as a relative baseline under the lighting and pose of this session, not a medical diagnosis.

A practical 30-day plan for the subject is: daily broad-spectrum SPF 50 outdoors; gentle cleansing morning and night; adequate sleep and hydration; and feature-specific grooming noted on each protocol page. Avoid aggressive actives until tolerance is clear.

Repeat analysis under consistent lighting, a neutral expression, and the same camera distance to compare progress. Discuss persistent or progressive concerns with a qualified clinician; this report does not replace clinical examination.
